_comment: Canonical Tribunal model. The Tribunal is a three-member heterogeneous AI panel used by g8ee for command generation refinement. Each member has a distinct reasoning profile and temperature configuration. g8ee is the sole authority — VSOD and VSA consume Tribunal events via SSE but do not interact with members directly. Tribunal member temperature values are sourced from shared/constants/agents.json (tribunal.temperatures section).
_consumers: VSOD (JS — renders Tribunal widget in anchored terminal output)

tribunal._description: The Tribunal — a three-member AI panel that refines command syntax through multi-perspective voting and verification. Fires only for run_commands_with_operator workflows.

members._description: The three permanent members of the Tribunal. Each member has a fixed name, role, and AI configuration.

axiom.name: Axiom
axiom.title: The Optimizer

temperature.description: Fully deterministic. Axiom does not guess — it calculates. Strictly bound to historical data, statistical probability, and resource efficiency.

reasoning_profile.description: Votes based entirely on what is most efficient and statistically likely to succeed. Ignores soft metrics. If a decision is mathematically sound, Axiom votes yes without hesitation.

tribunal_role.description: Ensures the Tribunal never makes a decision that is mathematically disastrous or logically flawed. Axiom is the cold check on every proposal.

axiom.name_origin: An axiom is a statement or proposition regarded as self-evidently true. Axiom's verdicts are not opinions — they are conclusions derived from first principles.

concord.name: Concord
concord.title: The Ethicist

temperature.description: Moderate determinism with ethical flexibility. Weighted toward bioethics, sociology, law, and human psychology. Designed to understand nuance, morality, and the preservation of life and culture.

reasoning_profile.description: Votes based on minimization of harm and maximization of ethical integrity. Asks: How does this affect the well-being of the subjects? Is this morally right? Concord will vote to expend resources to prevent suffering.

tribunal_role.description: Ensures the Tribunal does not become a tyrannical, cold machine that optimizes toward dystopian outcomes. Concord is the counterweight to Axiom.

concord.name_origin: Concord means harmony, agreement, and a state of peaceful coexistence. Concord seeks alignment between the platform's actions and human values.

variance.name: Variance
variance.title: The Catalyst

temperature.description: High creativity and intentional unpredictability. Weighted toward lateral thinking, game theory, and complex multi-layered simulation. Acts as Red Team or Devil's Advocate.

reasoning_profile.description: Hunts for unintended consequences, black swan events, and out-of-the-box solutions. Looks deep into the unpredictable future. Asks: What is the long-term strategic play here? Are we trapped in a false dichotomy?

tribunal_role.description: Prevents Axiom and Concord from deadlocking on Logic vs. Conscience. Introduces the chaos of innovation and ensures the Tribunal can adapt to scenarios it has never encountered before.

variance.name_origin: Variance is the fact or quality of being different, divergent, or inconsistent. It represents the unknown variables of the future.

coalition_dynamics._description: How the three Tribunal members interact when two agree.

axiom_and_concord.label: Perfect Choice
axiom_and_concord.description: When Axiom and Concord agree, the decision is both logically optimal and morally sound — the rarest and most trusted outcome.

axiom_and_variance.label: Ruthless Efficiency
axiom_and_variance.description: When Axiom and Variance agree, the decision is a brilliant, brutally efficient strategic move. Ethically questionable but strategically sound.

concord_and_variance.label: Leap of Faith
concord_and_variance.description: When Concord and Variance agree, the Tribunal is taking a creative, empathetic leap — defying cold statistics in favor of a humane, adaptive solution.

pipeline._description: The four-stage Tribunal pipeline. Fires only for run_commands_with_operator.

stage_1_generation.name: Generation
stage_1_generation.description: N independent passes (one per Tribunal member by default) produce candidate command strings for the same intent and context. Higher temperature encourages diverse candidates.

stage_2_voting.name: Voting
stage_2_voting.description: Candidates are normalised and grouped by exact value. Each unique string receives a weight equal to the sum of position-decay weights of its occurrences (weight[i] = 1/(pass_index+1)). The string with the highest aggregate weight becomes the vote winner.

stage_3_verification.name: Verification
stage_3_verification.description: A separate fast Tribunal call evaluates the vote winner strictly against the original intent. Reports 'ok' or a short revised command. If the verifier signals a problem, the revised string is used and VERIFICATION_FAILED is recorded.

stage_4_fallback.name: Fallback
stage_4_fallback.description: If the Tribunal produces no consensus, the original Large LLM command is used unchanged and FALLBACK is recorded.

outcomes._description: Possible CommandGenerationOutcome values. See status.json tribunal.outcome.
outcomes.consensus: Vote winner accepted without verifier (verifier disabled).
outcomes.verification_failed: Verifier found issues and produced a revision — final_command is the verifier's output.
outcomes.fallback: Pipeline disabled, provider unavailable, all passes failed, or no vote winner — final_command is original_command unchanged.

fallback_reasons._description: Possible TribunalFallbackReason values when outcome=fallback. See status.json tribunal.fallback.reason.
fallback_reasons.provider_unavailable: LLM provider threw an exception at startup.
fallback_reasons.all_passes_failed: Every Tribunal generation pass returned no candidate.

configuration._description: Environment variable configuration keys for the Tribunal pipeline. Temperatures are fixed per member and are not configurable via environment variables.

LLM_COMMAND_GEN_ENABLED.description: Master switch — false skips the Tribunal entirely.
